slides.title: Then and Now Vocabulary
slides.content:
The world has changed a lot! Let's learn some words:

✈️ airplane — a machine that flies in the sky

✉️ letters — messages written on paper and sent by post

🌐 Internet — connects computers all around the world

📱 tablet — a flat computer you can hold in your hands

📡 communications — ways to send and receive messages

📰 news — new information about what is happening

slides.title: Simple Past: Regular Verbs
slides.content:
To talk about the past, we add -ed to regular verbs:

walk → walked
play → played
listen → listened
visit → visited
change → changed

We use the same form for ALL subjects: I walked, she walked, they walked.
slides.tip: If the verb ends in 'e', just add 'd': change → changed, live → lived.

slides.title: Spelling Rules for -ed
slides.content:
How to add -ed correctly:

📌 Most verbs: just add -ed
walked, played, listened

📌 Verbs ending in 'e': just add -d
changed, lived, loved

📌 Verbs ending in consonant + 'y': change 'y' to 'i' and add -ed
carry → carried, study → studied

📌 Short verbs ending in consonant-vowel-consonant: double the last letter
stop → stopped, plan → planned

slides.title: Add -ed to These Verbs
slides.problem:
Write the past form of these verbs:

1. walk → ___
2. change → ___
3. visit → ___
4. play → ___
steps: 2️⃣ change → ends in 'e', just add -d → changed

slides.title: Life Long Ago
slides.html:
Grandma told Tom about life long ago.

'When I was young, people traveled by horse,' she said. 'There were no cars!'

'People listened to the radio for news because there were no televisions. Children played outside all day.'

'How did people talk to friends far away?' asked Tom.

'We wrote letters!' said Grandma. 'It was very slow. Now you use the Internet and tablets. Everything changed because of communications.'

'I visited other countries by boat,' said Grandma. 'Now people fly in airplanes!'

slides.title: Transport: Then and Now
slides.content:
How did people get around in the past?

🐴 Long ago, people walked or used horses.

🚂 Then trains arrived. People traveled by train.

🚗 Later, cars appeared. People loved cars!

✈️ Airplanes changed everything. Now people can fly anywhere in the world!

Today we have buses, trucks, motorcycles, cable cars and the metro too.

slides.title: Past or Present?
slides.problem:
Choose the correct form of the verb:

1. Long ago, people (travel / traveled) by horse.
2. Now, children (play / played) with tablets.
3. Grandpa (listen / listened) to the radio when he was young.
slides.answer: 1. Long ago, people traveled by horse. 2. Now, children play with tablets. 3. Grandpa listened to the radio when he was young.

slides.title: How We Talk Today
slides.html:
A hundred years ago, people walked to their neighbours' houses to talk to them.

Then someone invented the telephone. People called each other on the phone. They talked and listened for hours!

Later, people used computers. They typed emails to friends all over the world.

Now we have smartphones and tablets. We can video call anyone! The Internet changed communications forever.

People visited other countries more easily because of airplanes. The world feels smaller now!

slides.title: Time Words for the Past
slides.content:
We use special words to talk about the past:

📅 yesterday — the day before today

📅 last week / last year — the week or year before this one

⏳ long ago — a very long time in the past

⏳ in the past — before now

Example: Yesterday, I walked to school.
slides.tip: When you see time words like 'yesterday' or 'long ago', use the past tense!

slides.title: Write About the Past
slides.problem:
Complete the sentences using the past form (-ed):

1. Yesterday, I ___ (walk) to school.
2. Last year, we ___ (visit) London.
3. Long ago, people ___ (listen) to the radio.
slides.answer: 1. Yesterday, I walked to school. 2. Last year, we visited London. 3. Long ago, people listened to the radio.

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: ⏳ Vocabulary: airplane, letters, Internet, tablet, communications, news
📝 Simple Past regular verbs: add -ed (walked, played, listened, visited, changed)
🔤 Spelling: verbs ending in 'e' just add -d (changed, lived)
🚌 Transport changed: horses → trains → cars → airplanes
📡 Communications changed: letters → telephone → email → Internet
🗓️ Time words for the past: yesterday, last week, long ago, in the past
